seo.title: Building AI Chatbot with LangGraph + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build ai chatbot with LangGraph + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building AI chatbots with LangGraph enables complex, stateful conversational agents with retrieval-augmented generation, intent classification, and multi-turn memory. However, cyclical workflows and parallel state updates introduce risks like infinite loops, state corruption, and hallucinated responses. Vex provides runtime verification to ensure response accuracy, safety, and coherence in production deployments.

architectureOverview.heading: AI Chatbot Architecture with LangGraph
architectureOverview.description:
The LangGraph chatbot follows a cyclical graph pattern with nodes for intent classification, RAG retrieval, response generation, and safety checks. State is managed via a shared `ChatState` schema that persists conversation history, retrieved context, and metadata across turns. Parallel branches handle tool calls and human-in-the-loop approvals before merging into the main response path.

Intent classification routes user input to RAG (for knowledge queries) or tool nodes (for actions), with conditional edges preventing infinite loops. Checkpoints save state after each turn, enabling memory across sessions. Human-in-the-loop breakpoints allow review of sensitive responses before final output.

The graph uses `StateGraph` with persistent checkpointers (SQLite/Postgres) for production-scale deployments, handling 1000s of concurrent conversations while maintaining coherence.

risks.name: Cyclic execution loops
risks.description: Intent classification errors cause infinite routing between RAG and tool nodes
risks.mitigation: Vex monitors state transition depth and blocks loops > 10 cycles

risks.name: State corruption on merge
risks.description: Parallel RAG/tool branches overwrite conversation history inconsistently
risks.mitigation: Atomic state updates with Vex schema validation before checkpointing

risks.name: Hallucinated RAG responses
risks.description: LLM generates facts outside retrieved context, breaking accuracy
risks.mitigation: Vex semantic similarity checks between context and response

risks.name: Bypassed safety breakpoints
risks.description: Conditional edges skip human-in-the-loop for PII/safety violations
risks.mitigation: Vex runtime assertions enforce breakpoint execution

implementation.title: ChatState Schema Definition
implementation.description: Shared state schema with conversation history and RAG context for multi-turn coherence
implementation.code:
from typing import Annotated, TypedDict
from langgraph.graph import add_messages, StateGraph
from langgraph.graph.message import add_messages

class ChatState(TypedDict):
    messages: Annotated[list, add_messages]
    context: str
    intent: str
    metadata: dict

    def __init__(self):
        self.messages = []
        self.context = ''
        self.intent = 'unknown'
        self.metadata = {}

print('ChatState schema ready for graph')

implementation.title: Intent Classification Node
implementation.description: Routes conversations to RAG or tools based on user intent
implementation.code:
from langchain_openai import ChatOpenAI

llm = ChatOpenAI(model='gpt-4o-mini', temperature=0)

async def classify_intent(state: ChatState) -> ChatState:
    last_message = state['messages'][-1].content
    
    intent_prompt = f"""Classify intent: {last_message}
    Return only: 'rag' | 'tool' | 'greeting'"""
    
    intent = await llm.ainvoke(intent_prompt)
    return {'intent': intent.content.strip()}

print('Intent node functional')

implementation.title: RAG Retrieval Node
implementation.description: Fetches relevant context from vector store for grounded responses
implementation.code:
from langchain_community.vectorstores import FAISS
from langchain_openai import OpenAIEmbeddings

embeddings = OpenAIEmbeddings()
# Assume pre-built index
vectorstore = FAISS.load_local('rag_index')

async def retrieve_context(state: ChatState) -> ChatState:
    query = state['messages'][-1].content
    docs = vectorstore.similarity_search(query, k=3)
    context = '\n'.join([doc.page_content for doc in docs])
    
    return {
        'context': context,
        'metadata': {'sources': [doc.metadata for doc in docs]}
    }

print('RAG node ready')

implementation.title: Core Chatbot Graph Assembly
implementation.description: Compiles nodes into cyclical workflow with conditional routing
implementation.code:
from langgraph.checkpoint.sqlite import SqliteSaver

checkpointer = SqliteSaver.from_conn_string(':memory:')

graph = StateGraph(ChatState)

# Add nodes
graph.add_node('classify', classify_intent)
graph.add_node('retrieve', retrieve_context)
graph.add_node('respond', respond_with_context)  # defined elsewhere

# Conditional edges
def route_intent(state):
    return state['intent']

graph.add_conditional_edges('classify', route_intent, {
    'rag': 'retrieve',
    'tool': 'tools',
    'greeting': 'respond'
})

graph.set_entry_point('classify')
app = graph.compile(checkpointer=checkpointer)

print('LangGraph compiled successfully')

vexIntegration.title: Wrap graph.invoke() with Vex.verify()
vexIntegration.description: Runtime verification of final responses for accuracy and safety
vexIntegration.code:
import vex

@vex.verify(
    checks=[
        'response-accuracy',
        'conversation-coherence',
        'safety-filtering',
        'pii-protection',
        'hallucination-detection'
    ],
    risk_profile='high'
)
async def safe_chatbot(config, user_input):
    thread_id = config.get('thread_id', 'default')
    
    input_message = {'messages': [{'role': 'user', 'content': user_input}]}
    
    result = await app.ainvoke(input_message, config={'configurable': {'thread_id': thread_id}})
    
    return result['messages'][-1].content

# Usage
response = await safe_chatbot({'thread_id': 'user_123'}, 'What is LangGraph?')
print(response)

vexIntegration.title: Vex Safety Node in Graph
vexIntegration.description: Dedicated graph node for inline verification before human-in-the-loop
vexIntegration.code:
import vex

async def vex_safety_node(state: ChatState) -> ChatState:
    response = state['messages'][-1].content
    
    verified = await vex.verify_text(
        text=response,
        checks=['safety-filtering', 'pii-protection', 'hallucination-detection'],
        context=state['context']
    )
    
    if not verified:
        state['messages'].append({
            'role': 'system',
            'content': 'Response blocked by safety filters'
        })
    
    return state

# Add to graph
graph.add_node('vex_safety', vex_safety_node)
graph.add_edge('respond', 'vex_safety')

vexIntegration.title: State Schema Validation Checkpoint
vexIntegration.description: Prevents checkpoint deserialization errors with Vex schema enforcement
vexIntegration.code:
import vex
from langgraph.checkpoint.sqlite import SqliteSaver

@vex.schema_validator
class ValidatedChatState(ChatState):
    """Vex-validated state prevents corruption"""
    pass

class VexCheckpointer(SqliteSaver):
    async def aget_tuple(self, config):
        state = await super().aget_tuple(config)
        
        # Vex validates before returning
        await vex.validate_schema(state, ValidatedChatState)
        return state
    
checkpointer = VexCheckpointer.from_conn_string('chatbot.db')
app = graph.compile(checkpointer=checkpointer)

print('Vex-validated checkpointer active')
